name: Zerodha GraphQL Test Suite
scenario: The Zerodha GraphQL API has zero tests. Any schema change risks breaking client apps. Build a comprehensive GraphQL test suite using @GraphQlTest and GraphQlTester covering queries, mutations, subscriptions, and error scenarios.

core.description: @GraphQlTest(ZerodhaController.class) test class. @Autowired GraphQlTester. @MockBean for StockService and OrderService.
Error test: unknown stock → .execute().errors().satisfy(errors -> assertThat(errors).isNotEmpty()). No .errors().verify() on error tests.
Subscription test: livePrice subscription → .executeSubscription().toFlux().take(3).mapNotNull(r -> r.toEntity(PriceUpdate.class)).collectList().block(Duration.ofSeconds(5)). Assert size=3.
GraphQL test documents in src/test/resources/graphql-test/: stockBySymbol.graphql, createOrder.graphql, allStocks.graphql, livePrice.graphql.
README: explains @GraphQlTest vs HttpGraphQlTester (unit vs integration), and why .errors().verify() must come before .path() assertions.

bonus.description: HttpGraphQlTester integration test: @SpringBootTest(RANDOM_PORT). WebTestClient + HttpGraphQlTester.create(). At least 2 tests using real HTTP stack.
Security test: @WithMockUser for authenticated query. Anonymous user query returns error. Public query passes without auth.
DataLoader test: verify(@MockBean TradeRepository, atMost(1)).findBySymbols(argThat(list -> list.size() == 3)) — one batch call for 3 positions.

ai_policy_explanation: 5 hints. errors().verify(): ALWAYS call this before any .path() assertions — without it, GraphQL errors are silently ignored and the test passes even when the resolver threw an exception. documentName('name'): maps to src/test/resources/graphql-test/name.graphql — the file must exist. Use inline .document(gqlString) for quick tests without files. Subscription test: .executeSubscription().toFlux().take(N) is required — without .take(N), the Flux never completes (subscriptions are infinite streams). .variable() for every required argument: GraphQL will reject the request with a validation error if required variables are missing. entityList(Type.class) for list responses: .path('fieldName').entityList(Type.class).hasSize(N).
